sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, carb behavior is everything.
sections.subtitleText: 0.4g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, carb behavior is everything.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 31.9g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 31.9g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1.3g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 1.3g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 20% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 20% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 14% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 14% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage. preparation changes outcomes a lot because what you do to a tuber can make or break it. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Green Plantain does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
